Petition For Instructions Approving Settlement Agreement And Mutual Release; Instructions The Trust Shall Be Governed By The Settlement Agreement To The Extent That Settlement Is Inconsistent With The Terms Of Trust And Restatement; Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement Under Cal. Civ. Pro Code Section 644.6
Off Calendar Aug-19-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: David Mansour appearing by CourtCall for David Bassan. Michael Spalding appearing by Zoom for Lou Ann Bassan. Hong Chew (GAL) appearing in person. Sheila Robello appearing by CourtCall for Debra Dolch. Nathaniel Bassan (Beneficiary) appearing by Zoom.

The Court stated that it had signed an ex parte advancing this matter noting that the Minor is now an adult. Mr. Chew stated that he received the signature on Friday. The Court addressed Nathaniel Bassan stating that when this process started that he was under eighteen, that Mr. Chew represented him and his siblings, that he is over eighteen now, and that he had signed approving the settlement. Nathaniel confirmed for the Court that he approved the settlement. Ms. Robello stated that they are in the process of getting it filed. The Court was provided a copy of the settlement agreement by Mr. Chew. The Court addressed CCP 664.6 noting that the parties are requesting that it retain jurisdiction. All parties requested that the Court retain jurisdiction. The Court congratulated the parties on settling the case.
The Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Ex Parte Application For An Order Advancing Hearing Date On Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement, Or In The Alternative, For An Order Advancing Hearing Date And Shortening Time For Hearing On Approval Of Settlement Agreement; Declaration In Support

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar Jun-24-2020 Continued To Jul-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Mansour appeared by telephone for David Bassan and specially appearing for Attorney Michael Spalding.
Sheila Robello appeared by telephone for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee) also by telephone.
William Taylor appeared by telephone for Sharon Huang.

The Court noted that there is a settlement agreement in which the terms were agreed on in 2019, and that Mr. Chew was not present. The Parties reported that settlement has been in the works for almost one year, that they hope to finalize the terms this week and circulate it for signature, and that Ms. Robello is working on/has a draft of a petition for approval of the settlement agreement. The Parties also expressed concern about how far out the matter will be set, stated that there are many moving parts including obtaining of a bridge loan/refinance for which the offer expires in mid-August, and requested that a hearing date be set 40-days from now. The Court explained to Counsel that they need to file their petition and get whatever hearing date is assigned, and that they can then file an ex parte petition to advance that date. The Petition to Determine Invalidity of Trust Instrument, etc., the Amended Petition for Determination of Beneficial Interests in Trusts, etc., and the Petition to Compel Trustee to Provide Information, etc. are continued to July 22, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on filing of a petition for approval of the settlement agreement.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar Jan-22-2020 Continued To Apr-01-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee).
David Mansour for David Bassan (Petitioner).
William Taylor for Sharon Huang (Beneficiary).
Hong Chew Guardian Ad Litem for the Bassan Children.
Louann Bassan in Propria Persona.

Ms. Robello stated that they had two mediation dates, that they have a term sheet, that they are working on minor issues and that once the issues are resolved, they will petition for approval of the settlement. Mr. Mansour stated that it could take a couple of weeks to finalize things and requested a six to eight-week continuance. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Instrument, etc. is continued to April 1, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar Nov-13-2019 Continued To Jan-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Mansour for David Bassan and specially appearing for Attorney Michael Spalding and Attorney William Taylor.
Sheila Robello and Jessie Burgueno for Debra Dolch (Successor-Trustee).
Hong Chew for the five Bassan Children.

The Court questions how Ms. Dolch became the Successor Trustee. Ms. Robello stated that the trust is not under Court supervision for all purposes, that it is Court supervised just for the litigation and that pursuant to the terms of the trust there was a resignation and the appointment of Ms. Dolch. Mr. Mansour stated that they have had two mediations, that they were in settlement and were waiting for sale of one asset, that the sale went through and that the trust was injected with much needed cash. Mr. Mansour also stated that they are continuing negotiations and working on settlement, that discovery is on pause for six months and requested a 60 to 90-day continuance. Ms. Robello stated that she would like to keep it to a 60-day continuance. The Petition to Determine Invalidity of Trust Instrument, etc. is continued to January 22, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar Oct-16-2019 Continued To Nov-13-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar Jul-24-2019 Continued To Oct-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar Apr-17-2019 Continued To Jul-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Monsour for David Bassan (Petitioner) and specially appearing for Attorney Zak Taylor on behalf of Sharon Bassan (present) and specially appearing for Attorney Michael Spalding on behalf of Louann Bassan (present).
Natalie Lupo for Debra Dolch (Trustee).
Hong Chew Guardian Ad Litem for the Minor Children (Nathaniel, Eleanor, Celia, Hamilton and Morrison).

Counsel reported that they still have not resolved the issue of how Mr. Chew should be paid, that Mr. Monsour feels it should come out of the trust, and that Mr. Monsour, Mr. Taylor and Mr. Spalding all agree to continue the matter to do private mediation, but the Trustee does not agree. Ms. Lupo stated that the appropriate thing to do is four hours of pro bono mediation and if it goes beyond that, they can split the cost evenly. Ms. Lupo also raised the issue of the Trustee not being allowed to inspect the house and not being allowed to marshal the assets. Mr. Monsour stated that he does not feel inspection is necessary, that the house is not being sold and that his Client lives there with his five Children. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than July 17, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by July 19, 2019. The Petition to Determine Invalidity of Trust Instrument, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on July 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Ex Parte Application For Order For Appointment Of Guardian Ad Litem
Off Calendar Mar-04-2019 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar Dec-12-2018 Continued To Apr-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello and Natalie Lupo for Debra Dolch (Successor Trustee).
David Mansour for and with David Bassan (Petitioner).
William Taylor for Sharon Huang (Petitioner).
Michael Spalding for and with Louann Bassan (Beneficiary).

Ms. Robello stated that she is also appearing for Attorney Ruth Sullivan. Mr. Mansour stated that he circulated a stipulation for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL"), that he has not received it back, that the point of contention is how the GAL will be compensated and that his position is that the GAL be paid from the trust. Ms. Lupo stated that she objects to Mr. Chew being appointed, that she has asked for information regarding his background and that she wants to make sure Mr. Chew has experience in probate. The Court noted that if the parties don't agree on Mr. Chew, they can each have a Guardian Ad Litem, and if and when they agree on someone, they can file an ex parte petition. Mr. Taylor stated that his Client (the Mother) is not adverse to the Children. Ms. Robello stated that they received the stipulation yesterday, that the issue is about payment, that the Father has an issue with providing for the Children, raised the issue of all of the parties that are not objecting having to pay for the GAL, and requested that if the petition is not successful, that it be an offset to the Father's estate. Mr. Mansour also stated that discovery is ongoing, that he has subpoenaed records, that depositions will be scheduled early next year and proposed setting a trial date in 120-days. Mr. Taylor stated that Counsel for the Trustee and his Client have reached a tentative settlement. The Court indicated that it will not set a trial date at this time. The Petition to Determine Invalidity of Trust Instrument, etc., Amended Petition of Lou Ann Bassan for a Determination of Beneficial Interests in Trusts, etc. and Petition of Sharon Huang Bassan to Compel Trustee to Provide Information, etc. are continued to April 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status. If the issue of a Guardian Ad Litem is not resolved, the Court expects to have a petition for appointment filed by then.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Continued To Dec-12-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar Jul-23-2018 Continued To Nov-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-18-301644 the Maurice Bassan 2004 Trust; PTR-18-301659 The Maurice Bassan 2004 Trust dated Nov. 3, 2004 and PTR-18-301782 The Maurice Bassan Trust dated 11/3/04.

Appearances: Michael Spalding for and with Lou Ann Bassan.
David Mansour for and with David Bassan.
William Taylor appeared by telephone for and with Sharon Huang Bassan (present).
Kevin Lachona for Troyann Nielsen (Trustee).

The Court informed the Parties that they need to file an ex parte petition to consolidate in all three case numbers. Mr. Mansour raised the issue of appointment of the Parents as Guardian Ad Litem for the Minor stating that there is a conflict. Mr. Spalding ask the Court for a list of Guardian Ad Litems. The Court informed Mr. Spalding that it has no such list and suggested picking an Attorney that the Parties can all agree to. Mr. Mansour stated that discovery is ongoing, that they are meeting and conferring, and suggested setting a trial in November or doing mediation. The Petition to Determine Invalidity of Trust Instrument, etc., Petition of Lou Ann Bassan for a Determination of Beneficial Interests in Trusts, etc. and Petition of Sharon Huang Bassan to Compel Trustee to Provide Information are continued to November 19, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar Jun-11-2018 Continued To Jul-23-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-18-301644 The Maurice Bassan 2004 Trust; PTR-18-301659 The Maurice Bassan 2004 Trust dated Nov. 2004 and PTR-18-301782 The Maurice Bassan Trust dated November 3, 2004 as Amended and Restated by First Amendment July 10, 2015.

Appearances: William Taylor for Sharon Huang.
Kevin Lachona for Troyann Nilsen.
David Mansour for David Bassan.
Louann Bassan in Propria Persona.

No one objects to the matters being consolidated. Mr. Mansour will file an ex parte petition to consolidate the cases. The Parties addressed the issue of whether or not a Guardian Ad Litem needs to be appointed to protect the interest of the Minors. Mr. Taylor as well as Mr. Mansour expressed that without seeing the earlier Trust document, it is premature to determine whether there is a conflict of representation. Mr. Lachona stated that he does not have to provide the document under the probate code and that he has a signed Court order indicating that he does not have to turn over the 2004 document. Mr. Mansour stated that said order was for a different petition (request for production of documents), and that they have filed a contest. Mr. Lachona stated that he will talk to his Client. Mr. Taylor stated that the real issue is whether the house that the Children and his Client live in should be sold. All Parties waive notice of the Petition of Sharon Huang Bassan to Compel Trustee to Provide Information, etc. The Petition to Determine Invalidity of Trust Instrument, etc., Petition of Lou Ann Bassan for a Determination of Beneficial Interests in Trusts, etc. and Petition of Sharon Huang Bassan to Compel Trustee to Provide Information are continued to July 23, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to discuss where they want to go from here, whether they want to try mediation and whether the assets could be transferred from the 2003 Trust to the 2004 Trust.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Reporter: Vicki Haines, CSR #5995, 415-676-7416.

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Trust Instrument; For Removal Of Trustee For Breach Of Trust And Hostility; And To Instruct Trustee Not To Spend Trust Funds On Attorneys Fees In This Action
Off Calendar May-21-2018 Continued To Jun-11-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Instruct Trustee To Provide Copy Of Trust; To Compel Trustee To Provide Requested Information; And For Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Apr-04-2018 Advanced From Apr-04-18 To Mar-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Instruct Trustee To Provide Copy Of Trust; To Compel Trustee To Provide Requested Information; And For Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Apr-05-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Monsour for David Bassan (Petitioner).
Michael Spalding for and with Louann Bassan.
Ruth Sullivan for Troyann Nilsen.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not the Trustee has a duty to keep the Beneficiaries informed, the Trustee's duty of loyalty to the Beneficiaries, whether the Trustee is in breach of her duty by not providing documents to the Beneficiaries, whether the Trustee is refusing to provide information to protect herself, the Beneficiaries having a right to view documents and make an informed decision to file a contest, information being needed on the two trusts in order for the Beneficiaries to know if they should proceed with litigation, whether or not the prior Trust has to be produced when the trust has been restated and the original Trust is superseded, etc. The Petition to Instruct Trustee to Provide Copy of Trust, etc. is taken under submission. Both sides are to provide proposed orders.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: B. Wan. Reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR #4609, 415-272-1753.

Petitioners Ex Parte Application For Order Advancing Hearing Date And Shortening Time For Notice Of Petition
Off Calendar Mar-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
